large increase in fiber sometimes you can get a little bit clogged so just make sure that you're eating the vegetables in the prescribed amounts and if you're still doing that and still having an issue just add a little bit of psyllium husk or like metamucil something like that to your diet you can add them to your shakes there it's flavorless it really just makes us a little bit more mealy and then usually that solves any of those types of problems I'm having trouble eating all the food what should I do awesome so if you're having trouble eating all the food like I said you just want to a protein source it would also be a fat source and so if you had that in a meal that you didn't have a fat you'd have to adjust the rest of your day which can get really really confusing for people so I'm so tired of eating chicken every meal can I mix and match? Awesome question so any of these portions of fat, carbs or protein you don't have to have that full portion so what I mean by that is if it's a six egg whites if you want to have one portion of protein you could have three egg whites and then half a portion of another protein so let's say you wanted to have six egg whites and three-quarter cup of cottage cheese on the side you could do something like that so the normal serving of cottage cheese is a cup and a half normal serving of egg whites is six egg whites so all we did is a cup both of those in half and we combined them to make one full portion of protein. The same thing works for the carbohydrates if you want to do half a cup of black beans and half a cup of rice instead of one full cup of rice or one full cup of black beans all that's going to be the same so if you want to mix and match two or three different things to make a more you know have more variety in your meals totally totally okay the other thing to look at is make sure you're using the recipe book because we have a lot of cool recipes in there that don't take a lot of time and are easy to make on a budget and the other thing is use your spices getting tired of chicken is something that you don't want to do because I remember I had an old bodybuilding coach and he said I just don't allow myself to get tired of egg whites because what else would I have for breakfast every day so there's also a certain amount of mental I guess I don't want to say toughness but just understanding that some things aren't going to be milkshakes you know it's not always going to be delicious but it's going to be what your body needs and it's me great fuel so think about what you're gaining instead of what you're losing by dieting I'm a vegetarian are beans and quinoa good enough no they are not good enough they're actually still three-quarter carb sources to every gram of protein so stick to the amount of the to the proteins that are on the list if you are a vegan or you are a vegetarian it's really just going to be purposely cutting down on the selection of protein sources that you will have so that being said additional protein powders that are hemp protein powders rice protein powders things like that if you're a vegetarian or a vegan those are things that can help if you are not any of those things then don't worry about that stuff now that being said if you are a vegetarian or vegan protein is gonna be the hardest thing to get in because animal sources are where a lot of the protein sources that we have there lean and cheap come from so what we want you to do is if you're gonna if you're going to be a vegetarian if you're gonna choose to follow a vegetarian diet during this program then make sure that your carb sources and your fat sources are things that are a little bit more protein and so if you have your protein source make sure that the carb sources that you have are going to be your legumes your beans things like that that do have some additional protein that's not going to be a substitute for protein but it's going to help you hit your goal same thing with your fats stay a little bit more away from the oils lean more towards the nuts that are gonna have some tag along proteins and those things over over the course of the day will help you hit your overall protein goals if sometimes you get a little bit tired of let's say yogurt for breakfast can I use a shake instead of a normal protein for a meal great question so in a bind absolutely use a protein source for a meal a protein shake for a meal that being said is it something that you want to do on a regular basis having like three or four shakes a day not really just because you have micronutrients and things that real food is gonna have that a shake isn't all right so in a in a in a bind absolutely use the shake it's better than you know going off plant the other point to that is that if for some reason you are in a bind you don't necessarily need to eat right then if you're a little bit hungry you're not gonna die all right you can wait a couple hours and get home and then eat the right meal so just don't let that be the reason that you go off plant so I just wanted to address that and then it went from the shake standpoint if you need to have a shake you know once or twice or three times a week at most probably as a third meal instead that's okay that's fine make sure that you're still eating your other two meals I can't do dairy can I have the protein shakes instead of dairy if you can't have dairy then don't eat the dairy portions that are on the escape for you like just go continue and one of the important pieces that you need to learn how to make this a lifestyle because if you can't go away for three or four days and without going totally haywire and then losing the momentum that you have this is never gonna stick so it's really important to learn because the number one reason that people fall off diets is because of injury because of vacations so if you can learn how to follow through with that and stay on track the leg that you succeed in the long run which is why almost everyone's here is much much higher my schedule changed I won't be able to make my normal class time can I come to another time absolutely just make sure that the facility has the capacity to deal with it with with you at a different time most of the gyms that we work with are super flexible with that so it shouldn't be a problem and just ask any of the trainers no I'll answer that for you I haven't been losing weight should I cut out all my carbs no if you haven't been losing weight the first thing that we're gonna check is to make sure that you're actually eating everything that we need you to eat all right so that doesn't mean like you know three or four days this week I was really really on point you don't need to change your diet if you've been following it three or four days out of seven need to follow it a hundred percent of the time because if you just follow it even 70% of time you're not gonna get 75% of the results you'll get none of the results and it's unfortunate but that's just the reality of the way it works so you need to follow it a hundred percent of the time in the portions outline so it's not just eating like I'm eating everything that's on the list we use the same list when we're trying to help someone gain weight so it's not like it's a magic food list it's knowing how much and when and specific to you which is why you have a personal mail plan with the amounts that are specific to what you need to be eating should I eat only organic food no you don't need to eat only organic food it's gonna hurt your pocketbook a little bit if it's something that you're passionate about by all means go for it but it's not gonna any way affect your weight loss overall over the six weeks or even over the long term they've done a few meta-analyses on this stuff and they have yet to see a really big difference between eating normal food and eating organic should I cut out all of my salt should you cut out all of your salt no you need to keep salt in your diet just don't change what you're doing with your salt intake you're gonna be sweating especially if you're doing your challenge during the summertime you need to have sodium there's a lot of important processes for metabolism for mobilizing fat that includes sodium alright so you need to have salt same thing with performance when you're exercising salt actually helps you perform better it's something that's I don't want to say something that's good for you but it's not bad for you as long as you're exercising you're eating a normal diet most of times we have all these extra sodiums are coming in from pre-packaged foods things that are already prepared or have like long shelf lives if you're eating normal fresh food salt intake is not gonna be a problem now the last couple days before you actually have your way out we'll give you a little strategy to make sure that you don't you're not retaining an extra water weight so that doesn't affect your goal but that being said salt's not unhealthy for you as long as you're eating it in moderation how much water should I drink we recommend that everyone who's doing the challenge drink about a gallon of water a day it's about a half a fluid ounce per pound of body weight ideally but if if you go more than that we highly encourage it because you're gonna be sweating during your workouts you need to stay hydrated to stay focused you could you can Google benefits of staying hydrated and you'll have a list way longer than what I'm gonna get into right now but it's just the basics making sure hydrated making sure you're sleeping enough making sure actually eating the meal plan making sure working out three days a week you follow those things you won't have anything to worry about can I juice juicing so some people ask if the amount that you're juicing is the same amount of vegetables that you would normally have eaten and then you choose to juice those vegetables instead that's fine the issue that can come up with juicing let's say on a regular basis and having a massive juice like this is that even vegetables have sugar in them and when you're hat like most people could not eat the amount of vegetables that they're having to make a juice that's this big so it's about moderation we prefer you just eat your vegetables during this program if you really if you're already someone who juices we need you to probably take out the sugary things that you're eating and just keep it to vegetables in your juice and then in an amount that you probably be able to see on a plate which means that you're not gonna have a cup of juice that's like this it might be much smaller okay just make sure the whole purpose is this we're just trying to limit the amount of additional sugar that's coming in that's beyond we've already prescribed to you with your carbohydrate intake I want to tone my blank should I do extra blank that I read in X magazine or my friend told me to do who knows a personal trainer that they met one time um sorry this is just something we get a lot so there's there's really no such thing as spot reduction I mean as much as it sells and it sounds sexy like yeah if you want to reduce that on your stomach just do a thousand setups it doesn't really work that way you're gonna lose fat in the order that the reverse order that you gained it so let's say and this actually leads to another question which is you know if I lose weight can I can I not lose it for like women asses can I not lose it from my chest and can I just lose it from here unfortunately you can't choose where you lose it just like you couldn't choose where you gained it wherever you gained it is where you're gonna lose it first alright so when it comes to spot reduction it's still gonna come down to the fundamentals of losing fat all over your body and then eventually that piece will come out I promise you it just depends on when you gained it in that order if you gained all of your weight in your breasts for example and your arms were already you know had some fat on them beforehand realities that you'll very likely lose the fat in your breasts first before you lose the fat in your arms there's some people who had talked about breast I'll switch the stomachs let's say you had weight in your stomach and then you gained weight in your stomach over a certain amount of time that's the first place you're gonna lose it if you want to lose it on your legs it'll come after that there's one other question that I don't want to answer better my carbs that one okay can you can you just ask that question again can she yeah I haven't been losing weight should I cut out all of my carbs okay no you should not cut out all of your carbs making huge drastic changes like that is not what you want to do on this program so there's a couple things that if you if you are having trouble losing weight talk to your accountability coach and we'll take you through the steps that you need to go through all right that being said the first thing that we're going to check on is to make sure that you're actually following the diet a hundred percent now a lot of times it'll feel like a hundred percent but if you really think about it hasn't been a hundred percent you're like no I've been pretty good pretty good isn't and I'm not trying to be hard on you here it's just reality is that if you follow your diet 75% of the time you are not gonna get 75% results for none of the results and that's where a lot of people get into trouble with weight loss programs is that I'm just feeling like I'm putting all this effort in and I'm not seeing the results and it's usually because that 25% is putting them way above their surplus because one meal can ruin a week and so as much as I'd love to say like yeah if you just follow everything you know 75% you get 74 it just doesn't work that way so should you cut out your carbs no you should talk to your accountability coach and they'll take you through the steps that you need to make adjustments on your meal plan all right so don't make any changes without talking to your accountability coach
